---
title: AVL / textur
subtitle: "no"
format: html
# tmeta: $testmeta$
date: '`r format(Sys.time())`'
title-meta: ""
cmeta: |
  class: [16416] Text und Textur. Vom Webstuhl bis zu Large Language Models ::
  tutor: jan lietz ::
  term: SS26
  snc: 16162.1  
#[<< back to online paper <<](https://esteeschwarz.github.io/DC-LX/essais/papers/010/)  
#  arkiv: [<< the anarkkiv lankalevikki worksheet collection >>](https://stschwarz.userpage.fu-berlin.de/links/?title=paperfoot&ref=paper-temp)
arkiv: 16162.tiny
# foot: the anarkkiv lankalevikki worksheet collection >> <https://ada-sub.dh-index.org/blog>
cfoot: |
  [duumy foot collection](https://stschwarz.userpage.fu-berlin.de/links/?title=paperfoot&ref=paper-temp)
---


#### snc
[to commit](https://github.com/esteeschwarz/SPUND-LX/commit/{{< meta git-sha >}})

# index
Q: https://www.amaliesmith.dk/thread-ripper / 2020. Kopenhagen.
translated (eng): 2022, Jennifer Russel. London.  

## samling
> I intend to take pictures of plants and feed them to a machine-learning algorithm, which will then generate images of new digital vegetation based on the images I feed it. 
It knows what it means to be an image from the inside out, with no regard for the outside in.   
Working together with the machine-learning algorithm, I will weave and unravel artificial plant images on screen. A *Flora digitalica*, I told the committee. (2022, p.10)

### vector space of plants
- embeddings: where in an n-dimensional vector space representing (nature) is one specific plant located?
